title: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element ...
review_summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.
request_text: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pages/Dashboard.js move the top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser read into a useEffect inside the Dashboard component. Touch only those two files. Do not create new files. Apply the patch.

translation_json.normalized_request: Fix P69-10: Delete array element with id 'master1' from src/data/mockUsers.js, and move top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser read into a useEffect inside src/pages/Dashboard.js component. Touch only those two files.
translation_json.objective: Remove hardcoded user 'master1' from mockUsers.js and refactor Dashboard.js to read currentUser from localStorage inside useEffect instead of at top-level to fix caching issues showing previous logged-in user.
constraints: Delete element with id 'master1' from mockUsers.js array
Move localStorage.getItem currentUser read into useEffect in Dashboard.js
requested_outputs: Modified src/pages/Dashboard.js with localStorage read moved to useEffect

plan_json.objective: Implement the Jira issue by generating code changes, applying patches, running tests, and reviewing results.
plan_json.request_summary: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pag...
plan_json.change_summary: Remove the 'master1' user entry from src/data/mockUsers.js array and refactor src/pages/Dashboard.js to read currentUser from localStorage inside a useEffect hook instead of at the top-level of the module.
plan_json.change_explanation: The top-level localStorage.getItem call was causing caching issues where the previous logged-in user would display. Moving this read into a useEffect ensures the value is retrieved after the component mounts, preventing stale state.
assumptions: The referenced Jira issue exists and contains enough context to generate code.
Affected source files can be read from the knowledge index or sandbox.

tools.purpose: Generate code changes that implement the Jira issue requirements.

steps.title: Analyze P69-10 issue and identify affected files
steps.expected_output: Identified src/data/mockUsers.js (contains master1 element at lines 21-25) and src/pages/Dashboard.js (contains top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser call). Confirmed deletion target and refactoring requirements.
steps.success_criteria: Both files located with clear understanding of required modifications: remove master1 element and move localStorage read to useEffect

steps.title: Generate code patch for both files
steps.expected_output: Patch file containing: (1) Deletion of the array element with id 'master1' from src/data/mockUsers.js, and (2) Movement of localStorage.getItem('currentUser') from top-level to inside a useEffect in src/pages/Dashboard.js
steps.success_criteria: Patch is syntactically valid, touches only the specified two files, and addresses both requirements without creating new files

steps.title: Apply patch and run tests in sandbox
steps.expected_output: Both files modified successfully. Sandbox tests run and pass confirming no syntax errors or breaking changes.
steps.success_criteria: Patch applied cleanly, no conflicts, tests pass in sandbox environment

steps.title: Review generated changes
steps.expected_output: Code review confirms: (1) master1 element removed from mockUsers.js array, (2) localStorage read now occurs inside useEffect in Dashboard.js, (3) no unintended side effects or new files created
steps.success_criteria: Changes match specifications exactly, code quality standards met, only the two specified files were modified

review_json.summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.

policy_checks.name: tool_scope:codegen.generate_patch
policy_checks.detail: codegen.generate_patch is registered under codegen with write access.
